hypothesis: At fixed polymer and surface compositions, chain length, temperature, and interaction strengths, the polymer–surface binding free energy exhibits a nonmonotonic dependence on the ratio of polymer sequence autocorrelation length to surface pattern correlation length, with the strongest adhesion (most negative ΔF) occurring near ξp/ξs = 1 and weaker adhesion for ξp/ξs ≪ 1 or ≫ 1.
